rationale: Despite an M.S. in Computer Engineering from PES University and intermediate Spark/Redis skills (32+ months each), the entire career is in marketing and operations at TCS and Globex with no ML, IR, or retrieval engineering work anywhere in the profile.

Operations Manager at Stark Industries (fictional company) with only 1.2 years of experience, a marketing-manager background, M.E. in Physics, and no ML or information retrieval skills relevant to this role.

HR Manager at Stark Industries (fictional company) with a marketing-focused career description, no production ML or engineering work, and 2.0 years total experience with zero signal in IR, embeddings, or ranking systems.

Backend/full-stack engineer primarily at Accenture (consulting) and Stark Industries (fictional company) who lists Qdrant as a skill but only via a self-directed RAG side project, with no production ML, ranking evaluation, or hybrid retrieval experience.

Claims advanced Vector Search (44 months) and PyTorch (59 months) but all career descriptions show Android mobile and full-stack web development at Wayne Enterprises and Hooli (both fictional companies) plus Infosys and HCL (IT services), indicating keyword inflation with no verifiable IR or ranking production work.

IIIT Bangalore graduate with advanced LLM fine-tuning (34 months) and Qdrant experience plus a Zomato product-company stint, but 43 of 52 career months are at Tech Mahindra (IT services) with primary skills in speech recognition and TTS rather than IR or ranking evaluation.

6.6 years across product companies (Mad Street Den, Freshworks, Razorpay) building production recommendation and predictive systems with XGBoost, collaborative filtering, Weaviate vector DB, and PEFT fine-tuning, though explicit NDCG/MAP ranking evaluation ownership and BM25/hybrid retrieval are not evidenced.

Entire 10-year career in non-technical roles (Content Writer, Marketing Manager, Graphic Designer, Civil Engineer) at IT-services firms TCS and Wipro, with zero ML or engineering production work despite a Ph.D in Data Science that is wholly contradicted by the job history.

IIT Roorkee graduate with 5.4 years of data engineering (Airflow, Spark, dbt) and intermediate MLOps experience plus 30% ML work (sklearn/XGBoost) in one role, but no production IR, embeddings, or ranking systems experience and a role at Stark Industries (fictional company) is a red flag.

Marketing Manager at Infosys for 4.8 years with no engineering background; claims advanced Vector Search with only 3 endorsements in 12 months — textbook keyword inflation from a non-technical IT-services profile, exactly the anti-persona the JD warns against.
